title: Fixing Inconsistent CSS Border Radius Styles

statement.markdown: Given a CSS stylesheet, fix the bug in the border-radius utility function to correctly apply the specified radius to all four corners of a box element. The bug causes the top-left and bottom-right corners to have a 0 radius, while the top-right and bottom-left corners have the specified radius.
statement.inputFormat: A single string containing the CSS stylesheet with the buggy border-radius utility function.
statement.outputFormat: The corrected CSS stylesheet with the fixed border-radius utility function.
statement.notes: The corrected function should apply the specified radius to all four corners of the box element.

examples.explanation: The corrected function applies the specified radius to all four corners of the box element.
The corrected function applies the specified radius to all four corners of the box element.

hints.text: Review the CSS border-radius property and its syntax.

editorial.title: Fixing the Border Radius Utility Function
editorial.intuition: The corrected function should apply the specified radius to all four corners of the box element.
editorial.bruteForce: One possible approach is to manually update the border-radius property in the CSS stylesheet.
editorial.optimalSolution: The optimal solution involves updating the border-radius utility function to correctly apply the specified radius to all four corners of the box element.
editorial.proofOfCorrectness: The corrected function correctly applies the specified radius to all four corners of the box element.
commonMistakes: Mistaking the border-radius syntax or forgetting to update all corners.
